milestone.title: v4.0 — Corpus Expansion and Stratified Validation Corpus
milestone.description: Expand the Lincoln Metaphor Analysis project from the current 28-document curated corpus to a larger, stratified corpus. Add a 48-document fully annotated core corpus, define a 75–100 document extended validation corpus, and establish a search-only reference corpus layer. V4 will implement corpus selection rationale, document metadata, provenance tracking, ingestion templates, segmentation, sentence-ID stability, corpus validation, coverage reports, expansion impact reports, updated reliability sampling, updated publication-package language, and release checklist. The goal is to reduce selection-bias risk while preserving auditability and interpretive depth. Tracked by #134; tracking issues use the tracking label and must be updated as child issues close.

milestone.title: v3.0 — Human Inter-Annotator Reliability Study
milestone.description: Add a blind two-human inter-annotator reliability study for the Lincoln Metaphor Analysis project. V3 will generate human coder packets, training materials, structured annotation templates, schema validation, agreement metrics, disagreement logs, adjudication queues, adjudication decisions, codebook revision notes, and publication-ready reliability documentation. This milestone establishes the project’s first true human-human reliability layer while preserving Stage 4A annotations, Stage 4B AI-assisted reliability results, and Stage 4M multi-model AI stress-test artifacts as separate methodological layers. Tracked by #108; tracking issues use the tracking label and must be updated as child issues close.

V3 will generate human coder packets, training materials, structured annotation templates, schema validation, agreement metrics, disagreement logs, adjudication queues, adjudication decisions, codebook revision notes, and publication-ready reliability documentation. This milestone establishes the project’s first true human-human reliability layer while preserving Stage 4A annotations, Stage 4B AI-assisted reliability results, and Stage 4M multi-model AI stress-test artifacts as separate methodological layers. Tracked by #108; tracking issues use the tracking label and must be updated as child issues close.

milestone.title: v2.0 — Multi-Model Reliability Stress Test
milestone.description: Add a v2 reliability layer that packages blind annotation tasks for multiple AI models, ingests model outputs, computes agreement and disagreement metrics, categorizes unstable coding fields, generates a model-consensus report, and integrates the results into the publication package without overwriting Stage 4A reference annotations. This milestone strengthens methodological transparency while preserving the distinction between AI-assisted reliability stress testing and human inter-annotator reliability. Tracked by #85; tracking issues use the tracking label and must be updated as child issues close.
